diary: Sunny Monday always feels like the city’s exhaling a little. I woke up in Renton and did my quick stretch before the swim at the YMCA in Horizon View—laps first, then that calm cool-down where my shoulders finally stop feeling “on duty.” The drive into downtown was smooth enough, even with the light rail closed between Westlake and University District; I stuck to the roads and got to the King County Courthouse early. Court prep is my comfort ritual: files aligned, notes checked, coffee in hand. After a quick bite near Pike Place at the Seattle Public Market, I spent the late morning handling client matters—serious, steady work. At 11am I slipped into The 5 Point Cafe in Capitol Hill for USA vs Wales, the whole place buzzing. Later, I grabbed groceries at QFC in Seward Park, then headed to Issaquah City Hall for more prep. Volunteering at Seattle Union Gospel Mission was the soft landing of the day, and salsa class in Bellevue gave me a grin I could feel in my feet. Back home, I ate simple, stretched again, and let the day settle—pretty good, honestly.
thought_process: I planned my day around three things: keeping court prep tight, compensating for the light rail closure, and letting the morning match be a genuine break instead of another distraction. I drove in early so I wouldn’t be stressed by transit gaps, and I kept errands clustered—coffee and breakfast near Pike Place, groceries at QFC on the way back east—so my mental bandwidth stayed for client calls. The World Cup airing at 11am PDT pulled me downtown to The 5 Point Cafe, but it also gave me an easy social moment with low stakes. I could feel my mood lift during the match, then settle back into focus for Issaquah City Hall. Volunteering at the Union Gospel Mission anchored everything—tired, but purposeful—so by evening salsa, I was ready to move instead of just think.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
